analysis_result: MEK_UP.V1_UP (MSigDB C6): NES = -1.097, FDR q-value = 0.654 in MPNST-G1 vs MPNST-G2 comparison (positive ranking stat = higher in G1). The pathway is not significantly enriched (FDR > 0.25) and trends toward enrichment in G2 (negative NES).
denominator_used: MPNST-G1 n=6 (M803, M1014, M2252, M2346, M2372, M3048) vs MPNST-G2 n=6 (M1677, M2325, M2377, M2491, M4719, M9413); GSE207400 log-normalized bulk RNA-seq matrix with 14,992 genes after dropping NaN; MEK UP.V1 UP gene set with 173 genes (31 in leading edge); gseapy prerank permutation_num=1000.
assumptions_made: Used Welch's t-statistic (G1 - G2) as the pre-ranked gene metric, higher=up in G1; MSigDB Oncogenic Signatures (C6) library accessed via gseapy get_library; sample M1933 (MPNST-G2) was excluded because it is not present in the GSE207400 expression matrix (6 vs 6 groups); gseapy prerank with seed=42, min_size=10, max_size=1000, 1000 permutations; matched clinical Tumor_ID to matrix column names directly.
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
   - GSE207400_Matrix.txt.gz: 14,995 genes x 48 samples (log-normalized expression).
   - 41467_2023_38432_MOESM4_ESM.xlsx (Clinical Data): 108 rows x 15 columns.

B. COHORT DEFINITION
   - MPNST_G1 with RNA-Seq='X' and Tumor_ID present in matrix -> 6 samples: M803, M1014, M2252, M2346, M2372, M3048.
   - MPNST_G2 with RNA-Seq='X' and Tumor_ID present in matrix -> 6 samples: M1677, M2325, M2377, M2491, M4719, M9413 (M1933 excluded; not in matrix).

C. VARIABLE CODING
   - Matched clinical Tumor_ID directly to GSE207400 column headers.
   - Ranking stat: Welch t-stat per gene = ttest_ind(G1, G2, equal_var=False), positive = higher in G1.

D. COMPUTATION
   - Dropped genes with NaN t-stat -> 14,992 ranked genes.
   - gseapy.prerank(rnk, gene_sets='MSigDB_Oncogenic_Signatures', seed=42, permutation_num=1000, min_size=10, max_size=1000).
   - Filtered output for term 'MEK UP.V1 UP'.

E. RESULT
   - MEK UP.V1 UP: ES = -0.247, NES = -1.097, NOM p-val = 0.273, FDR q-val = 0.654, FWER p-val = 1.000, Tag %: 31/173.
   - Interpretation: not significant; modest negative enrichment (trending toward higher in MPNST-G2).

F. CAVEATS
   - Small sample sizes (6 vs 6) limit power and inflate permutation-based p-value variance.
   - One MPNST-G2 sample (M1933, RNA label RT1933) was absent from the released matrix and had to be dropped; the published figure may have used it, potentially shifting NES/FDR somewhat.
   - gseapy prerank implements the Broad GSEA algorithm but exact results can differ from broad GSEA java by ~5-10% on FDR due to permutation sampling.
   - Ranking metric choice (Welch t-stat) is a standard default; other choices (signal-to-noise, log2FC) can produce different NES magnitudes.
